interactions.description: Methotrexate + Trimethoprim: both are antifolate drugs. Co-administration causes severe pancytopenia.
interactions.clinical_effect: Additive bone marrow suppression, potentially fatal pancytopenia
interactions.recommendation: Avoid co-administration. If unavoidable, monitor CBC twice weekly.

interactions.description: Hydroxychloroquine + Amiodarone: both prolong QT interval. Combined use increases risk of fatal arrhythmia.
interactions.clinical_effect: Additive QT prolongation, risk of Torsades de Pointes
interactions.recommendation: Avoid co-administration. Monitor ECG if clinically necessary.

interactions.description: Rifampicin + OCP: Rifampicin induces CYP3A4, reducing contraceptive efficacy to near-zero.
interactions.clinical_effect: Loss of contraceptive efficacy, unintended pregnancy
interactions.recommendation: Use alternative contraception (IUD, depot injection) during and for 4 weeks after rifampicin.

interactions.description: Warfarin + Diclofenac: NSAIDs increase bleeding risk with anticoagulants via platelet inhibition and GI erosion.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs. Use paracetamol for analgesia.

interactions.description: Warfarin + Aspirin: dual antiplatelet/anticoagulant effect markedly increases bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid unless cardiology indication. Monitor INR closely.

interactions.description: Artemether/Lumefantrine + Efavirenz: both prolong QT. Efavirenz also reduces lumefantrine levels via CYP3A4 induction.
interactions.clinical_effect: QT prolongation and reduced antimalarial efficacy
interactions.recommendation: Monitor ECG. Consider dose adjustment or alternative antiretroviral.

interactions.description: Methotrexate + Diclofenac: NSAIDs reduce renal clearance of methotrexate, causing toxicity.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs with methotrexate. Use paracetamol.

interactions.description: Rifampicin + Warfarin: Rifampicin induces CYP enzymes, dramatically reducing warfarin levels.
interactions.clinical_effect: Loss of anticoagulation, risk of thrombosis
interactions.recommendation: Increase INR monitoring frequency. May need 2-3x warfarin dose. Consider alternative anticoagulant.

interactions.description: Rifampicin + Efavirenz: Rifampicin reduces efavirenz levels via CYP induction.
interactions.clinical_effect: Subtherapeutic antiretroviral levels, risk of HIV treatment failure
interactions.recommendation: Increase efavirenz to 800mg OD or use alternative TB regimen.

interactions.description: Amiodarone + Azithromycin: both prolong QT interval.
interactions.clinical_effect: QT prolongation, risk of Torsades de Pointes

interactions.description: Amiodarone + Warfarin: Amiodarone inhibits warfarin metabolism, increasing INR.
interactions.recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose by 30-50%. Monitor INR weekly.

interactions.description: Omeprazole + Methotrexate: PPIs may reduce renal clearance of methotrexate.
interactions.clinical_effect: Increased methotrexate levels, risk of toxicity
interactions.recommendation: Consider withholding PPI during high-dose methotrexate.

interactions.description: Prednisone + Diclofenac: combined GI toxicity risk.
interactions.clinical_effect: Increased risk of GI bleeding and peptic ulceration
interactions.recommendation: Co-prescribe proton pump inhibitor if combination unavoidable.

interactions.description: Metoclopramide + Morphine: Metoclopramide counteracts opioid-induced gastroparesis.
interactions.recommendation: May be used intentionally. No dose adjustment needed.

interactions.description: Rifampicin + Omeprazole: Rifampicin induces CYP2C19, reducing omeprazole efficacy.
interactions.clinical_effect: Reduced acid suppression, treatment failure for peptic ulcer
interactions.recommendation: Increase omeprazole dose or use H2 blocker.

interactions.description: Hydroxychloroquine + Azithromycin: both prolong QT interval.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor ECG. Avoid in patients with pre-existing QT prolongation.

interactions.description: Rifampicin + Prednisone: Rifampicin induces steroid metabolism, reducing efficacy.
interactions.recommendation: Double prednisone dose when co-administered with rifampicin.

allergies.description: Penicillin allergy: contraindication to all penicillin-class antibiotics (J01C*)
Penicillin allergy with cephalosporin cross-reactivity: ~1-2% cross-reactivity rate with 1st-gen cephalosporins
NSAID allergy: contraindication to all NSAID-class analgesics
